channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Middle East latest: Israeli hostages' families welcome news that 3 more men will be released
item.description: The Israeli organization representing families of hostages held in the Gaza Strip celebrated the news Friday that three high-profile male hostages would be released by Hamas the following day. In turn, Palestinian authorities say Israel has agreed to release 90 prisoners.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Serbian protesters march ahead of their Danube bridge blockade as driver rams Belgrade demonstration
item.description: INDJIJA, Serbia (AP) — Hundreds of striking students marched through the Serbian countryside Friday as they took their anti-graft protest toward the northern city of Novi Sad, where they intend to blockade three bridges over the River Danube this weekend.
item.dc:creator: ELDAR EMRIC and DUSAN STOJANOVIC, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Iran's covert nuclear agency found operating out of top space program launch sites
item.description: Sources embedded in Iran uncover "large scale" personnel and operations affiliated with Iran's nuclear program are now operating out of two of Tehran's top space development and testing sites.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Rwanda-backed rebels move deeper into eastern Congo as UN reports executions and rapes
item.description: GOMA, Congo (AP) — Rwanda-backed rebels were expanding their presence in eastern Congo after capturing Goma, the region's major city, the U.N.
item.dc:creator: CHINEDU ASADU and JUSTIN KABUMBA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Panama president: 'Impossible' to negotiate over canal
item.description: Panama President José Raúl Mulino rejected negotiations over ownership of the Panama Canal, in the face of President Trump’s fixation on the U.S. retaking the trade waterway.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Growing anger in Greece over deadly rail disaster prompts plan by opposition to challenge government
item.description: Three Greek opposition parties say they are planning to challenge the conservative government with a censure motion over its handling of a 2023 rail disaster that killed 57 people.
item.dc:creator: Derek Gatopoulos, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Expected release of high-profile male hostages sparks excitement and anxiety in Israel
item.description: News that three high-profile hostages are expected to be released has brought excitement and trepidation to Israel on Friday.
item.dc:creator: Julia Frankel, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Russia claims another Ukrainian village has fallen as it closes in on a key city
item.description: Russia is claiming it has captured another village in its relentless offensive in eastern Ukraine’s Donetsk region and as it closes in on the critical Ukrainian logistics hub of Pokrovsk after almost three years of war.
item.dc:creator: Illia Novikov, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Lithuanian president backs Trump’s NATO defense spending goal amid ongoing Russian threat
item.description: VILNIUS, Lithuania (AP) — Securing an end to the fighting in Ukraine must take place with Kyiv's full involvement and come with more defense spending by regional countries to avoid any future Russian aggression, according to Lithuania's president.
item.dc:creator: LIUDAS DAPKUS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Pakistan's favorable trade status depends on progress in human rights, media freedom, EU says
item.description: The European Union has warned Pakistan that its status as a duty-free exporter to the bloc will continue to depend on the progress the country makes in addressing concerns about civil and labor rights and freedom of speech.
item.dc:creator: Munir Ahmed, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Tens of thousands of Muslims attend annual Biswa Ijtema event in Bangladesh
item.description: TONGI, Bangladesh (AP) — Tens of thousands of people gathered on a riverbank near Bangladesh’s capital on Friday to listen to sermons by Islamic scholars at the Biswa Ijtema, or global congregation of Muslim devotees.
item.dc:creator: JULHAS ALAM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Fear, disease and debt afflict Venezuelans released from prison after post-election arrests
item.description: TOCUYITO, Venezuela (AP) — Venezuela’s President Nicolás Maduro labeled them as terrorists on national television. They were plucked from pharmacies, apartment buildings and other locations, and thrown in prison for months.
item.dc:creator: REGINA GARCIA CANO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Israelis and Palestinians rejoice after more hostages and prisoners are freed
item.description: KHAN YOUNIS, Gaza Strip (AP) — Eight more hostages were freed from the Gaza Strip by Hamas-led militants on Thursday in a sometimes chaotic process that briefly delayed Israel's release of 110 Palestinian prisoners and underscored the fragility of the ceasefire that began earlier this month.
item.dc:creator: MOHAMMAD JAHJOUH, WAFAA SHURAFA and ISABEL DeBRE, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: An Iraqi man who carried out Quran burnings in Sweden is killed in a shooting
item.description: Salwan Momika, a 38-year-old Iraqi man who staged several burnings and desecrations of the Quran in Sweden in 2023, has been killed in a shooting near Stockholm.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Caribbean nation of St Kitts launches investigation after 19 people found dead in drifting vessel
item.description: Authorities in the Caribbean nation of St. Kitts and Nevis have launched an investigation after discovering a partially submerged boat containing at least 19 bodies.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Musk nominated for Nobel Peace Prize by European Parliament member
item.description: A European Parliament member from Slovenia says he nominated tech giant Elon Musk for a Nobel Peace Prize for his free speech advocacy. “The proposal that Mr.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Gisèle Pelicot's ex-husband, imprisoned for raping and drugging her, now caught up in other cases
item.description: NANTERRE, France (AP) — Dominique Pélicot, the convicted rapist who horrified France by drugging his then wife so other men could rape her, was questioned Thursday about other cases of rape and murder that he's suspected in.
item.dc:creator: NICOLAS GARRIGA and JOHN LEICESTER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: France hands over last military base in Chad
item.description: N’DJAMENA, Chad (AP) — France handed over its last military base in Chad to local authorities on Thursday, two months after the former French colony in central Africa broke off its defense cooperation agreement with Paris.
item.dc:creator: MOUTA ALI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Middle East latest: Hostage releases begin in Gaza Strip
item.description: Hamas handed captive Israeli soldier Agam Berger over to the Red Cross, the first of eight hostages set to be released today.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Woman used wigs, disguises to take British citizenship tests for at least 14 people, authorities say
item.description: A British woman used wigs and disguises to impersonate applicants taking citizenship tests across the United Kingdom, authorities said.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Rubio exempting 'life-saving humanitarian assistance’ from foreign aid restrictions
item.description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio issued a waiver on Tuesday exempting “life-saving humanitarian assistance” from President Trump’s executive order pausing nearly all foreign aid during a period of review. Trump’s executive order pausing funding, issued on his first day in office, suspended any new U.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, The Hill

item.title: Denmark to increase military spending in North Atlantic by $2 billion amid row with Trump over Greenland
item.description: Denmark is increasing military spending in the North Atlantic amid President Donald Trump’s bid to have Greenland sold or ceded to the United States.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump invites Israel's Netanyahu to meet with him at the White House next week
item.description: WADI GAZA, Gaza Strip (AP) — President Donald Trump has invited Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu to the White House next week as the first foreign leader to visit in Trump's second term, Netanhayu and the White House said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA and MELANIE LIDMAN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: South African elephant kills tourist who was trying to save children at Kruger National Park
item.description: A tourist at South Africa's Kruger National Park was trampled to death by an elephant as he was trying to protect children, officials say.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: EU military chief says it would make sense to put European troops in Greenland, Welt reports
item.description: Top-ranking European military official Robert Brieger reportedly said it would "make perfect sense" to station troops in Greenland amid President Trump's interest in acquiring it.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 22 reported killed in Lebanon before agreement to extend deadline for Israeli forces to withdraw
item.description: MAYS AL-JABAL, Lebanon (AP) — Israeli forces in southern Lebanon on Sunday opened fire on protesters demanding their withdrawal in line with a ceasefire agreement, killing at least 22 and injuring 124, Lebanese health officials reported.
item.dc:creator: KAREEM CHEHAYEB and ABBY SEWELL, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Colombian president orders increase of import tariffs on US goods after Trump order
item.description: Colombian President Gustavo Petro, in a post on the social platform X, said he ordered the "foreign trade minister to raise import tariffs from the U.S. by 25%.
item.dc:creator: Elvia Limon, NewsNation

item.title: Colombia to send presidential plane to Honduras to pick up migrants from US flights
item.description: President Gustavo Petro has arranged for the presidential plane to facilitate the "dignified return of Colombian nationals "
item.dc:creator: Elvia Limon, NewsNation

item.title: A child's life in Gaza, as explained by a Middle East reporter
item.description: Middle East reporter Arwa Damon, who founded the nonprofit INARA, described what she's seen in Gaza to NewsNation.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Soriano, NewsNation

item.title: Trump wants Egypt and Jordan to take in Palestinians from Gaza. Here's why they are likely to refuse
item.description: DUBAI, United Arab Emirates (AP) — President Donald Trump's suggestion that Egypt and Jordan take in Palestinians from the war-ravaged Gaza Strip is likely to be met with a hard “no" from the two U.S. allies and the Palestinians themselves, who fear Israel would never allow them to return.
item.dc:creator: JOSEPH KRAUSS, Associated Press, WGN 9
